name: Ostabat Loop
region_intro: Hiking around Ostabat-Asme offers access to the verdant Pyrenean landscapes of the northern Basque Country. The region is characterized by its valleys and surrounding mountains, serving as a significant point on the historic GR65, also known as the Way of St. James. This area provides diverse scenery, including forests and hills, making it a compelling destination for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Ostabat-Asme The most popular hiking route is Ostabat Loop, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This circular path features an ascent of 150 meters through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gibraltar Stele – Chapelle de Soyarza loop from Orsanco, a moderate 8.9 miles (14.4 km) path. This route includes a cumulative ascent of over 450 meters and offers varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Chapelle de Soyarza loop from Arhansus, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through the local hills, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Hiking around Ostabat-Asme is defined by its connection to the Way of St. James, its Pyrenean foothills, and picturesque valleys. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Ostabat-Asme are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Ostabat-Asme's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Ostabat-Asme?
A: Ostabat-Asme offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 60 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, including 21 easy, 33 moderate, and 9 difficult options, ensuring there's a path for every hiker.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Ostabat-Asme?
A: Hiking in Ostabat-Asme immerses you in the verdant Pyrenean landscapes of the northern Basque Country. You'll encounter picturesque valleys, rolling hills, and lush forests. The region is also known for its panoramic views, particularly from spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Iparlatze&lt;/a&gt;, offering vistas of the surrounding Basque mountain peaks.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking routes in Ostabat-Asme?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5346360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapelle de Soyarza loop from Arhansus&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.4 km long and typically takes under 2 hours to complete, leading through gentle local hills.

Q: What are some notable historical or cultural landmarks to see on a hike?
A: Ostabat-Asme is rich in history, especially due to its connection to the Way of St. James. You can explore the village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ostabat&lt;/a&gt; itself with its historic houses, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/930773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapelle de Soyarza&lt;/a&gt;, a 12th-century chapel. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Stele&lt;/a&gt; is another significant landmark, marking a historical gathering point for pilgrims.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in the area?
A: Yes, Ostabat-Asme offers several excellent circular routes. A well-regarded option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877281878/ostabat-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ostabat Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.4 km trail that takes around 2 hours and 20 minutes to complete. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38590074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Stele – Chapelle de Soyarza loop from Orsanco&lt;/a&gt;, which is a longer, moderate 14.4 km path.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ostabat-Asme?
A: The region's verdant Pyrenean landscapes are beautiful throughout much of the year. While specific recommendations depend on personal preference, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.tourisme64.com/en/our-destinations/welcome-to-the-basque-country/living-mountains/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basque Country&lt;/a&gt; is known for its diverse scenery accessible across seasons, so even winter can offer unique hiking experiences, especially in the Iraty Massif area.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ostabat-Asme?
A: The hiking trails in Ostabat-Asme are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the connection to the historic Way of St. James, and the beautiful Pyrenean foothills and valleys.

Q: Can I find routes that are part of the Way of St. James (GR65) in Ostabat-Asme?
A: Absolutely. Ostabat-Asme is a pivotal point on the historic GR65, also known as the Camino Francés or the Way of St. James. Many pilgrims begin their journey here or pass through, making it an ideal location to experience sections of this renowned pilgrimage route. For more information on this historic path, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.tourisme64.com/itineraire/ostabat-asme/ITIAQU064V508HRL-de-ostabat-a-saint-jean-pied-de-port/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tourisme64.com&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, the region offers more demanding options. While not a single-day loop, the multi-day journey from Ostabat-Asme towards Roncevaux along the GR65 involves a cumulative elevation gain of over 2,300 meters on its second leg, providing a significant adventure for experienced trekkers.

Q: Where can I find parking when planning a hike in Ostabat-Asme?
A: While specific parking locations for every trailhead are not detailed here, Ostabat-Asme is a well-known starting point for many pilgrims and hikers. Parking is generally available within the village itself, and at common starting points for popular routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877281878/ostabat-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ostabat Loop&lt;/a&gt; or routes originating from nearby Orsanco or Arhansus.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the wider Basque mountain peaks?
A: Yes, for breathtaking panoramic views, consider routes that pass through or near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Iparlatze&lt;/a&gt;. From this mountain pass, you can enjoy expansive vistas across the valley and the surrounding Basque mountain peaks, offering a true sense of the region's natural grandeur.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Ostabat-Asme?
A: The duration for moderate hikes in Ostabat-Asme can vary depending on the specific route and your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877281878/ostabat-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ostabat Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.4 km, typically takes around 2 hours and 20 minutes. A longer moderate option like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38590074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Stele – Chapelle de Soyarza loop from Orsanco&lt;/a&gt; (14.4 km) might take closer to 4-5 hours.
tips:
Ostabat is a very beautiful, old place with narrow streets and only 285 inhabitants. It is located directly on the GR65 and is only one stage away from St-Jean-Pied-de-Port.
Shortly before Ostabat, the GR65 goes through a narrow ravine and then climbs into the village, because Ostabat is on a hill and can therefore be seen from afar.
Ostabat was probably founded in the 10th century by the lords of Luxe and Lantabat and became an important stage of the pilgrimage to Saint-Jacques-de-Compostelle.
In the financial census of 1350 there were twenty inns, two hospitals and three churches in Ostabat. During this time, Ostabat was able to accommodate several thousand pilgrims per day.

Latsaga Castle (or Laxaga or Laxague) is privately owned and cannot be visited. Latsage Castle was built in the 14th century from a fortified building from the 13th century and remodeled at the end of the 15th century.
